We compare the two discount series for part (a) by calculating the net cost factor:  
For 30-10-2.5%: (0.70)(0.90)(0.975) = 0.61425, so discount ≈ 38.575%.  
For 25-15-2%: (0.75)(0.85)(0.98) = 0.62475, so discount ≈ 37.525%.  
Thus, 30-10-2.5% is better.  

For part (b), 15-15% gives (0.85)(0.85) = 0.7225, discount ≈ 27.75%, which is less than the single 30% discount.  
So the single rate of 30% is better.  

The correct choice is that the series 30-10-2.5% and the single rate of 30% are better, which matches option
